title: Selaginella myosuroides
uses: Selaginella myosuroides is known to have various medicinal properties. It is often used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. In some cultures, it is used to treat respiratory issues and as an antiseptic. More research is needed to fully understand the extent of its medicinal applications.
taxonomy: Selaginella myosuroides is a species of spore-producing plant in the genus Selaginella. It belongs to the family Selaginellaceae, which is a group of lycophytes. The Selaginellaceae family is part of the order Selaginellales, which is a group of vascular plants that reproduce via spores. These plants are further classified under the division Lycopodiophyta, a group of plants that are thought to be one of the earliest vascular plant lineages. Selaginella myosuroides is a type of resurrection plant due to its ability to survive in dry conditions, and its classification as a lycophyte indicates its ancient lineage.
